news.title: Mideast conflict lifts Brent back above $94, stocks give up gains
news.summary: Oil prices rose sharply on Wednesday as fresh US strikes triggered air defences in Iran, while stocks in Asia gave up earlier gains and inched higher in Europe.Mirroring overnight gains on the Nasdaq, Asia tech stocks rose for a second straight day, with Tokyo up almost two percent before closing slightly lower.

news.title: Stock Futures Fall as Inflation Fears Resurface
news.summary: Stocks were on track to open lower on Wednesday after a jump in oil prices revived fears that a flare-up in inflation could drag down the market.  Nasdaq 100 futures fell 0.8%.  Stocks looked set to give up some of those gains on Wednesday amid a rally in oil prices, after the U.S. launching strikes against Iran for an 11th straight night.

news.title: Tesla’s Earnings Are Coming. 3 Things to Watch For Before the Drama Begins.
news.summary: Tesla is set to report second-quarter earnings after Wednesday's market close.  For the period, Wall Street is looking for earnings per share of 55 cents and an operating profit of $1.7 billion from sales of $27.3 billion, according to FactSet.  Higher oil prices, Tesla incentives, and EV retrenchment from traditional car makers helped boost Tesla’s numbers.
